# Volume Economics

## The Physics

The cost per unit of any standardized activity decreases as volume increases.
Governance overhead is amortized: one standard applied to 100 ventures costs
less per venture than 100 bespoke governance systems. Reusable patterns,
shared templates, and common standards are economic advantages, not just
aesthetic preferences.

## Why It Matters

- **Governance ROI**: Investment in standards pays increasing returns as ventures multiply.
- **Template economics**: A library of proven patterns reduces startup cost for new ventures.
- **Shared infrastructure**: Common governance tooling serves all ventures from one investment.
- **Quality floor**: Standardized approaches establish a baseline that ad-hoc approaches cannot match.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Economies of scale (microeconomics foundational).
- Platform economics: the platform investment is amortized across all participants.
- Franchise model: proven patterns replicated at unit cost.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-P12 (Design Once, Compose Many) — the principle of reusable patterns
- JOS-C01 (Fractal Self-Similarity) — the same pattern everywhere reduces variety cost
